pois.name: Kaiserpfalz Goslar
pois.description: A Romanesque imperial palace complex built between 1040 and 1050, serving as a primary seat for the Salian emperors.

pois.name: Hildesheimer Dom
pois.description: The Cathedral of the Assumption of Mary is a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its medieval bronze Bernward Doors and the 1,000-year-old rose bush.

pois.name: St. Michaeliskirche Hildesheim
pois.description: An outstanding example of Ottonian architecture, this double-choired basilica features a world-famous painted wooden ceiling from the 13th century.

pois.name: Schloss Marienburg
pois.description: A magnificent Neo-Gothic palace built by King George V of Hanover as a summer residence and gift for his wife, Queen Marie.

pois.name: Neues Rathaus Hannover
pois.description: A castle-like city hall featuring a unique arched elevator and offering panoramic views of the state capital from its dome.

pois.name: Herrenhäuser Gärten
pois.description: One of the most distinguished baroque formal gardens in Europe, heritage of the House of Hanover.

pois.name: Fagus-Werk
pois.description: Designed by Walter Gropius, this shoe last factory is a landmark of early modern industrial architecture and a UNESCO site.

pois.name: Schloss Bückeburg
pois.description: The ancestral seat of the Princes of Schaumburg-Lippe, featuring a splendid Golden Hall and one of the world's largest private mausoleums.

pois.name: Rattenfängerhaus Hameln
pois.description: A magnificent Weser Renaissance building associated with the Pied Piper legend, now housing a historic restaurant.

pois.name: Schloss Hämelschenburg
pois.description: A fully preserved Weser Renaissance manor complex located on the pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela.

pois.name: Einbecker Brauhaus
pois.description: The birthplace of Bock beer; this brewery has been a cornerstone of German beer culture since the Middle Ages.

pois.name: Knochenhauer-Amtshaus
pois.description: Regarded as one of the most beautiful half-timbered buildings in the world, originally the guild hall of the butchers.

pois.name: Gänseliesel-Brunnen
pois.description: A landmark fountain in Göttingen where newly graduated PhD students from the university traditionally kiss the bronze girl statue.

pois.name: Weserstein Hann. Münden
pois.description: A memorial stone marking the point where the Werra and Fulda rivers join to form the Weser.

pois.name: Historisches Rathaus Duderstadt
pois.description: One of the oldest and most striking town halls in Germany, featuring three distinctive timber-framed towers.

pois.name: Welfenschloss Herzberg
pois.description: The largest half-timbered castle in Lower Saxony, once a residence of the Guelph dukes and known as the Esperanto city castle.

pois.name: Marktkirche zum Heiligen Geist
pois.description: The largest wooden church in Germany, built for the mining community of Clausthal from spruce and oak.

pois.name: Bergwerk Rammelsberg
pois.description: A UNESCO-listed mine that operated continuously for over 1,000 years, providing a unique record of industrial history.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Wolfenbüttel
pois.description: A baroque palace that served as the residence of the Dukes of Brunswick-Lüneburg and features a well-preserved facade.

pois.name: Burgplatz Braunschweig
pois.description: The historic center of the city featuring the Brunswick Lion statue, the Cathedral of St. Blaise, and the Dankwarderode Castle.

pois.name: Kaiserdom Königslutter
pois.description: A Romanesque basilica founded by Emperor Lothair III, known for its rich stone carvings and the tomb of the emperor.

pois.name: Juleum Novum Helmstedt
pois.description: The impressive main building of the former University of Helmstedt, showcasing late Renaissance architecture.

pois.name: Forschungsmuseum Schöningen
pois.description: Site of the discovery of the world's oldest preserved wooden hunting weapons, known as the Schöningen Spears.

pois.name: Kloster Wienhausen
pois.description: A Cistercian nunnery famous for its medieval tapestries and uniquely preserved interior from the Gothic period.

pois.name: Schloss Celle
pois.description: The largest castle in the southern Lüneburg Heath, featuring the oldest baroque theater in Germany still in use.

pois.name: Gedenkstätte Bergen-Belsen
pois.description: A solemn memorial site on the grounds of the former Nazi concentration camp, honoring the tens of thousands who died there.

pois.name: Sieben Steinhäuser
pois.description: A group of five Neolithic dolmens located within a military training area, accessible to the public on weekends.

pois.name: Platz Am Sande Lüneburg
pois.description: The central square of the Hanseatic city, flanked by magnificent brick-gabled houses that showcase the wealth from the salt trade.

pois.name: Kloster Ebstorf
pois.description: A former Benedictine nunnery home to a copy of the famous Ebstorf Map, the largest world map from the Middle Ages.

pois.name: Rundlingsdorf Lübeln
pois.description: A perfectly preserved example of a medieval 'Rundling' village layout, characteristic of the Wendland region.

pois.name: Hitzacker Weinberg
pois.description: A rare northern vineyard overlooking the Elbe river valley, located in a town known for its unique island-like old center.

pois.name: Dom zu Bardowick
pois.description: A massive three-aisled hall church built in the North German Brick Gothic style, far exceeding the size of the village.

pois.name: Hansehafen Stade
pois.description: The historic harbor of Stade, featuring a wooden treadmill crane and beautifully restored Hanseatic merchant houses.

pois.name: Barkenhoff Worpswede
pois.description: The former home and workshop of Heinrich Vogeler, central to the famous Worpswede artists' colony.

pois.name: Kugelbake Cuxhaven
pois.description: A wooden navigation landmark marking the point where the Elbe ends and the North Sea begins.

pois.name: Schloss Evenburg
pois.description: A Neo-Gothic moated castle in Leer surrounded by extensive parklands, representing East Frisian noble life.

pois.name: Kesselschleuse Emden
pois.description: A unique four-way lock where four different canals meet, allowing vessels to cross levels in multiple directions.

pois.name: Greetsieler Zwillingsmühlen
pois.description: Iconic twin windmills standing at the entrance to the picturesque fishing village of Greetsiel.

pois.name: Ludgerikirche Norden
pois.description: The largest medieval church in East Frisia, containing a world-famous Arp Schnitger pipe organ.

pois.name: Schloss Jever
pois.description: A former residence of the Lords of Jever, featuring an impressive onion-shaped tower and a precious carved ceiling.

pois.name: Friesisches Brauhaus zu Jever
pois.description: A traditional brewery renowned for its distinctive bitter-style Pilsner and modern automated production lines.

pois.name: Kaiser-Wilhelm-Brücke
pois.description: A grand swing bridge in Wilhelmshaven, once the largest of its kind in Europe, spanning the harbor inlet.

pois.name: Schloss Oldenburg
pois.description: A Renaissance and Baroque palace that served as the residence of the Counts and Grand Dukes of Oldenburg.

pois.name: Zwischenahner Meer
pois.description: The third largest lake in Lower Saxony, created by a collapsed salt dome and now a major leisure destination.

pois.name: Museumsdorf Cloppenburg
pois.description: The oldest open-air museum in Germany, featuring over 50 historic buildings that showcase rural life in the region.

pois.name: Varusschlacht im Osnabrücker Land
pois.description: The likely site of the Battle of the Teutoburg Forest in 9 AD, now a museum and park exploring Roman-Germanic history.

pois.name: Rathaus Osnabrück
pois.description: The site where the Peace of Westphalia was negotiated in 1648, ending the Thirty Years' War.

pois.name: Schloss Iburg
pois.description: A combined castle and monastery complex that was a residence of the Prince-Bishops of Osnabrück.

pois.name: Dümmer See
pois.description: A shallow lake located within a nature park, crucial as a habitat for migrating wetland birds.

pois.name: Koppelschleuse Meppen
pois.description: A historic lock on the Ems-Hase canal, now serving as a cultural center in a wooded park setting.

pois.name: Meyer Werft Besucherzentrum
pois.description: One of the world's leading cruise ship builders, offering insight into massive industrial shipbuilding in Papenburg.

pois.name: Steinhuder Meer
pois.description: The largest inland lake in Northwest Germany, located in the middle of a nature park.

pois.name: Inselfestung Wilhelmstein
pois.description: A man-made island fortress in Steinhuder Meer, built by Count William of Schaumburg-Lippe as a military defense.

pois.name: Stockturm Nienburg
pois.description: The last remaining tower of the former Nienburg Castle, once a residence for the Counts of Hoya.

pois.name: Dom zu Verden
pois.description: A hall church that is the first Gothic cathedral built in Lower Saxony, known for its fine proportions.

pois.name: Okertalsperre
pois.description: A large reservoir in the Harz mountains providing flood protection and hydropower, popular for boat tours.

pois.name: Beningaburg Dornum
pois.description: A well-preserved medieval chieftain's castle in East Frisia, reflecting the history of the Beninga family.

pois.name: Sielhafenmuseum Carolinensiel
pois.description: A museum dedicated to the historical coastal shipping and the unique harbor life of the East Frisian coast.

pois.name: Kloster Loccum
pois.description: A remarkably well-preserved Cistercian monastery that remained intact after the Reformation.

pois.name: Radaufall
pois.description: An artificial 22-meter-high waterfall in the Harz, created in 1859 to enhance the local tourist appeal.
